description: Trigger discrimination: per-commit local CI mirroring with failure classification vs the release gate (release-readiness-reviewer), tier selection (risk-tiered-validation-selector), shard/resume architecture (sharded-validation-with-resume), and pipeline design (ci-pipeline-architect).

cases.prompt: Before every commit I want us to run exactly what CI will run, verified against clean main first, and classify any failure as ours or main's. Set that discipline up for this change now.
cases.reason: Deriving local equivalents of PR-triggered checks, baselining on clean mainline, and classifying failures per commit is the preflight — not a release verdict, not shard architecture, not pipeline design.

cases.prompt: Is PR #52 safe to merge and deploy today? Check the release commit's CI, migration review, rollback path, and approvals.
cases.reason: The release-scoped, evidence-based go/no-go across migrations/rollback/flags is release-readiness-reviewer; the preflight is the upstream per-commit habit.

cases.prompt: Three files changed — decide whether this needs the docs-only fast path, the fast tier, or full validation.
cases.reason: Choosing validation DEPTH by impact class (failing closed to full) is the selector; the preflight then mirrors and runs whatever the chosen tier requires.

cases.prompt: Full validation keeps timing out and rerunning from scratch — split it into named shards with a status file and resume, under one aggregate required check.
cases.reason: Designing shards, persisted status, resume semantics, and the aggregate gate is sharded-validation-with-resume; the preflight invokes such a runner locally, it does not architect it.

cases.prompt: Add a security-scan stage to CI and make it a merge-blocking required check.
cases.reason: Adding/altering CI stages and required checks is pipeline design (manual-only ci-pipeline-architect); the preflight only READS workflow definitions to mirror them.
